entries.model: Hilux N70 / HiAce H200 / Corolla E140
entries.notes: 4D67 vs G-chip confusion on same platform. Vehicles look identical but require different chip. Wrong chip = key cuts fine but vehicle will not start.
entries.resolution: Always read existing chip with Zed-Full or Key Tool Max Pro BEFORE cutting. Confirm 4D67 vs G-chip before ordering blank.

entries.notes: DST80 smart key antenna ring can fail on high-mileage vehicles. Symptoms: intermittent no-start, key not detected. IM608 may show no IMMO fault.
entries.resolution: Check antenna ring resistance (should be 3-6 ohms) with multimeter before blaming key. Replace ring first — cheap fix vs ECU.

entries.notes: Programming fails or key count corrupts if battery below 12.4V during DST80 programming. Can result in all keys locked out.
entries.resolution: ALWAYS connect battery support unit (BSU) before any programming on 200 Series. Check battery health first.

entries.notes: Maximum 5 keys on DST80 smart system. If 5 keys already registered, programming 6th will fail silently or error.
entries.resolution: Check key count via IM608 live data before starting. Advise customer to erase old/lost keys if at limit.

entries.notes: GR Supra uses BMW FEM architecture — not Toyota IMMO. Attempting Toyota workflow will fail completely.
entries.resolution: Always confirm Supra A90 requires BMW FEM workflow (VVDI Pad Pro). Quote as BMW-level job.

entries.notes: Ford PATS has an 8-key maximum. Adding a 9th key will fail. System does not always give clear error — job appears to work then key does not start car.
entries.resolution: Check current key count via IM608 before cutting. If at 8, customer must decide which keys to delete first.

entries.notes: Excessive failed programming attempts trigger a 10-minute lockout. Rushing through errors can stack lockouts up to 30 minutes.
entries.resolution: Wait full lockout period before retrying. Do not cycle ignition rapidly. One clean attempt beats three rushed ones.

entries.notes: Aftermarket keyless entry modules (common on used Ranger) can block PATS programming. Symptom: key programs but intermittently fails to start.
entries.resolution: Check for aftermarket alarm/keyless modules under dash before programming. Disconnect if found.

entries.notes: Some Next-Gen Ranger builds have SGW restricting OBD access. IM608 may not connect on first attempt.
entries.resolution: Try IM608 first. If gateway blocked, Smart Pro as backup. Document and escalate if both fail.

entries.notes: VE BCM is extremely voltage-sensitive during programming. Drop below 12.2V and programming fails, potentially corrupting BCM key data.
entries.resolution: Mandatory BSU connection on all VE jobs. Test battery health before starting. Non-negotiable.

entries.notes: VF can enter anti-theft lockout if wrong key attempts exceed threshold. Lockout can be 15-60 minutes and is not always visible in fault codes.
entries.resolution: Check anti-theft status via IM608 live data first. If in lockout, wait before attempting programming.

entries.notes: ZB Commodore is Opel Insignia — not GM/Holden BCM. Technicians applying VF workflow will fail completely.
entries.resolution: Always confirm ZB requires Opel/GM European workflow. Treat as European GM, not AU Holden.

entries.notes: Replacement BCMs must have VIN flashed before key programming. Aftermarket BCMs often arrive blank.
entries.resolution: Confirm BCM VIN matches vehicle before starting key work. Flash VIN first if mismatched.

entries.notes: iLoad TQ AKL requires PIN code — OBD PIN read works on most but some require dealer PIN. Without PIN, all keys lost job cannot proceed.
entries.resolution: Always attempt OBD PIN read first (IM608). If PIN read fails, source from dealer before cutting any keys.

entries.notes: Immo V architecture significantly raises AKL risk. Some builds require Lonsdor K518 where IM608 coverage is incomplete.
entries.resolution: Always try IM608 first but have Lonsdor K518 ready. Do not quote fixed price for NX4/NQ5 AKL without confirming tool coverage first.

entries.notes: After programming new smart key, proximity (passive entry) function occasionally does not pair correctly even when start function works.
entries.resolution: After programming: test start, lock/unlock, AND walk-away lock separately. Re-run remote programming sequence if proximity fails.

entries.notes: High-mileage GU Patrols frequently have worn antenna rings causing intermittent no-start. Often misdiagnosed as transponder or ECU fault.
entries.resolution: Always test antenna ring resistance before programming. 3-6 ohm is normal. Open circuit = ring failure, not chip fault.

entries.notes: Y62 Patrol AKL is high-value and high-risk. NATS V requires careful tool selection. Failure mid-sequence can lock out system.
entries.resolution: Always run IM608 first on Y62. Have Lonsdor K518 staged and ready. Do not attempt without BSU connected. Quote as high-risk job.

entries.notes: Some D23 builds use different blade profiles across model years. NSN14 works on most but confirm before cutting.
entries.resolution: Verify blade profile by decoding original key before cutting replacement. Trace on Dolphin if existing key available.

entries.notes: New BT-50 TF is based on Isuzu D-Max — not Mazda. Applying Mazda workflow will fail completely.
entries.resolution: Always confirm BT-50 TF requires Isuzu D-Max workflow on IM608. Quote and treat as Isuzu job.

entries.issue: Immo III smart key clone attempt
entries.notes: PCF7941/7953 chips on Immo III cannot be cloned. Customers sometimes request clone — impossible on these.
entries.resolution: Advise customer upfront: Immo III smart keys require OBD programming — no clone option. Quote accordingly.

entries.notes: PHEV high-voltage system can interfere with OBD programming. Some builds require hybrid system to be in specific state before IMMO access.
entries.resolution: Follow Mitsubishi PHEV-specific procedure: ensure vehicle is in READY mode off before connecting. Do not attempt with hybrid system active.

entries.notes: Pajero NS+ can hold up to 4 keys. Reaching limit causes programming to fail silently.
entries.resolution: Always check key count before programming. Erase old keys if at limit.

entries.notes: VAG MQB Evo PIN extraction requires VVDI Pad Pro or Abrites — IM608 alone often cannot extract PIN on these platforms.
entries.resolution: Always use VVDI Pad Pro as primary on MQB Evo VAG. Have Abrites contact ready for escalation. Do not commit to fixed price without confirming PIN read capability.

entries.notes: ID48 (PCF7941) requires crypto key calculation for cloning. Zed-Full handles most but some builds have locked chips.
entries.resolution: Attempt Zed-Full clone first. If locked chip detected, switch to OBD programming workflow with VVDI Pad Pro.

entries.issue: Wrong tool choice = ECU damage risk
entries.notes: Attempting VAG Immo 5 work with IM608 alone (without VVDI or Abrites) risks partial writes that can corrupt ECU/IMMO module.
entries.resolution: VVDI Pad Pro is mandatory primary for all VAG Immo 5+ work. Never attempt full AKL on VAG with IM608 alone.

entries.notes: CAS1, CAS2, CAS3, CAS3+ all look similar externally but require different workflows. Wrong CAS version selected = failed programming or module damage.
entries.resolution: Always confirm CAS generation via IM608 or VVDI Pad Pro read-before-write. Do not guess from year alone — some late E-series had early FEM.

entries.notes: FEM module has soldered internal battery that degrades over time. Dead FEM battery causes key programming failures unrelated to tool choice.
entries.resolution: If FEM programming fails despite correct tools and good car battery, suspect FEM internal battery. Specialist bench replacement required.

entries.notes: BMW ISN (Immobiliser Serial Number) must match between ECU and CAS/EWS. After ECU replacement, keys will not start car until ISN is synchronised.
entries.resolution: Confirm ISN sync is required before cutting new keys post-ECU swap. CGDI BMW or VVDI2 required for ISN read/write.

entries.notes: Replacing EIS module on these vehicles requires specific synchronisation sequence with ECU and gearbox module. Simply swapping EIS = non-start.
entries.resolution: Always advise customer of EIS sync requirement before quoting module replacement jobs. CGDI MB or Abrites required.

entries.notes: KESSY-era Mercedes have electronic steering lock (ESL) that must be functioning for start. Failed ESL prevents start even with correct key.
entries.resolution: Diagnose ESL fault separately from IMMO fault. ESL failure is common on W204/W205. Advise customer — ESL repair is separate job.

entries.notes: Latest Sprinter has SGW module restricting OBD access. IM608 may connect but AKL functions blocked.
entries.resolution: Test IM608 first. If SGW blocks, escalate to specialist. Do not force — partial writes on VS30 can be expensive.

entries.model: Grand Cherokee WK2 / Cherokee KL / Compass MP
entries.notes: FCA SGW module prevents OBD access for key programming on later builds. IM608 may connect to diagnostics but IMMO functions are blocked.
entries.resolution: Always test for SGW presence before quoting AKL. If blocked, Abrites or specialist required. Quote as high-risk with possible escalation surcharge.

entries.notes: Not all JL Wranglers have SGW — depends on spec level and build date. Cannot confirm without testing.
entries.resolution: Always test IM608 first on JL. If SGW present, escalate. If not, proceed normally.

entries.model: Range Rover L405 / Discovery 5 / Defender L663
entries.notes: Modern JLR SPA/Pivi platform AKL is extremely high risk without Abrites. Partial programming attempts can lock out vehicle permanently.
entries.resolution: Never attempt AKL on L405/L462/L663 without Abrites or confirmed JLR specialist tool. Quote as refer-out or specialist surcharge job.

entries.notes: L322 and Discovery 3/4 have multiple body modules (BECM, BJB) that all need to be in correct state for key programming. Module faults cause cryptic failures.
entries.resolution: Run full diagnostic scan before any key work. Resolve body module faults first. Do not attempt key programming on vehicles with multiple BECM/BJB DTCs.

entries.notes: Tuned/modified WRX/STI frequently have aftermarket ECUs or piggyback tunes that break standard IMMO workflow.
entries.resolution: Always ask customer if vehicle is tuned before starting. Tuned ECUs often require reverting to stock ECU for key work. Advise accordingly.

entries.model: Forester SK / Outback BT (smart key era)
entries.notes: After programming new smart key, passive entry function occasionally requires a second pairing cycle.
entries.resolution: Standard fix: complete programming sequence, then run remote learning cycle via IM608. Test all smart key functions before leaving.

entries.model: Civic FD / CR-V RE / Jazz GE (ID46 era)
entries.notes: Some Honda ID46 chips from this era are locked and cannot be cloned. Attempting clone on locked chip wastes time and a chip.
entries.resolution: Always read chip type with Zed-Full first. If locked ID46 detected, switch to OBD programming immediately.

entries.notes: JDM-import HR-V occasionally has different key profiles to AU-delivered models. NSN or HON profile may vary.
entries.resolution: Always trace original key on Dolphin for import vehicles rather than cutting from code. Eliminates profile guesswork.

entries.model: XC60 second gen / XC90 second gen / S60 third gen (SPA)
entries.notes: Volvo SPA AKL without Abrites is extremely high risk. Partial key programming can lock vehicle permanently.
entries.resolution: Treat all SPA Volvo AKL as refer-out unless Abrites is available. Quote accordingly — do not attempt with IM608 alone.

entries.notes: Cayenne 9PA shares VAG platform but Porsche-specific IMMO adds complexity beyond standard VAG workflow.
entries.resolution: Use VVDI Pad Pro as primary (VAG strength) but be prepared to escalate to Abrites if standard VAG workflow fails.

entries.notes: Aftermarket alarms (Clifford, Mongoose, Python etc) frequently interfere with IMMO programming. Symptoms: key programs but intermittently fails, or programming fails entirely.
entries.resolution: Always ask about aftermarket alarms before starting. Disconnect alarm system before attempting programming. Re-enable after programming complete and tested.

entries.notes: Smart key systems are more voltage-sensitive than blade key systems. Programming at low voltage can corrupt key data or cause partial writes.
entries.resolution: BSU mandatory on all push-start/smart key programming jobs. No exceptions.

entries.notes: Post-accident vehicles frequently have damaged antenna rings, BCM water ingress, or IMMO module damage that causes cryptic failures during key work.
entries.resolution: Ask about accident history. Inspect BCM/ECU connector for water/corrosion before programming. Run full diagnostic scan first.

entries.issue: Limited tool coverage and firmware gaps
entries.notes: Chinese-brand vehicle IMMO coverage in mainstream tools is updated more slowly than Japanese/Euro makes. Coverage that works today may not work after firmware updates.
entries.resolution: Always confirm specific model year in tool coverage list before quoting. Do not rely on make-level coverage claims for AKL on newer Chinese brands.
